scenario_walkthroughs.critic_walkthrough: Independent trace: (1) detectMode() called — no profile file, no code indicators → detectedPath='pure_greenfield'. (2) resolveFlowMode('pure_greenfield', null) → mode='greenfield', onboardingRequired=false. (3) User runs full discover/spec/build via existing greenfield path unchanged. (4) After build, writeProfileFromArtifacts(rootDir, 'specs/', 'greenfield') called. (5) Loads discover.json, spec.json, build_report.json, decisions.json. (6) Calls extractL0/L1/L3 (L2 conditional on l2Enabled). (7) Calls writeLayer for each — auto-injects updated_at, validates schema, writes to .nopilot/profile/. (8) Returns layersWritten list. Code confirms: no specs/ mutation, only .nopilot/profile/ written.

scenario_walkthroughs.description: Add feature to existing project with profile
scenario_walkthroughs.critic_walkthrough: Independent trace: (1) detectMode() — l0-infra.json exists and parses → hasProfile=true → detectedPath='returning_project'. (2) checkStaleness() — compares l0.updated_at vs latest git commit timestamp, warns if delta > threshold. (3) resolveFlowMode('returning_project', 'feature') → mode='feature'. (4) discover.md: Feature Step 0 replaces Steps 0a-0d. Layer 1 skipped entirely (written as stub). Layer 2 inherits from profile. detectConflicts() called after requirements defined — compares feature requirements vs L2 philosophy/constraints and L3 domain_model entities. Conflicts presented with ADAPT/EVOLVE/DEFER options. resolveConflict() called with user choice; EVOLVE sets profileUpdateFlag=true. (5) discover artifacts written to specs/features/feat-xxx/. (6) spec.md reads L1 for existing module/interface context. (7) critic.md performs impact audit when L1 available. (8) build.md branches worktree from HEAD. (9) writeProfileFromArtifacts called with feature artifactsDir and mode='feature'. Domain model merged incrementally via mergeDomainModel.

scenario_walkthroughs.description: First-time onboarding of existing codebase (no profile)
scenario_walkthroughs.critic_walkthrough: Independent trace: (1) detectMode() — no .nopilot/profile/l0-infra.json but package.json and/or .git detected → hasCode=true, hasProfile=false → detectedPath='first_time_onboarding'. (2) resolveFlowMode('first_time_onboarding', null) → onboardingRequired=true. (3) discover.md mode detection block: scans codebase via scanCodebase() to generate L0/L1/L3. (4) Asks user: 'full project-level discover (greenfield) or add a feature (feature mode)?'. (5) User choice routes to appropriate flow. (6) Design principle 'Context must not overgeneralize' prevents first-feature philosophy from being written as whole-project philosophy. Confirmed in discover/index.json design_philosophy[4].

gaps_noted.gap: parallelization in scanCodebase is a flag only — actual sub-agent spawning is the caller's responsibility (prompt-level, not TypeScript)
gaps_noted.risk: INV-005 structural bound is enforced; actual parallelization is a prompt-level contract in discover.md mode detection block

gaps_noted.gap: handleStalenessResponse regeneration path (TEST-042) writes updated profile layers but the written L0 partial may be incomplete (scanCodebase does not produce full schema-valid L0 alone without updated_at injection from writeLayer)
gaps_noted.risk: writeLayer auto-injects updated_at so schema validation passes; partial data is acceptable per REQ-006-AC-3 which states scan supplements, not replaces

gaps_noted.gap: conflict-detector uses keyword-matching heuristics for philosophy/constraint conflicts, not LLM semantic comparison
gaps_noted.risk: Acknowledged in pre_mortem: 'Conflict detection false positives erode user trust.' Keyword matching is conservative (fewer false positives, more false negatives). Acceptable for MVP.
